search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG97 memory concern assessment recommendations
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Commercial tools do not establish the complete workflow source.
Treatment and medicine content was not mapped.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for adults with suspected or diagnosed dementia; the legacy workflow has no adult restriction and includes undifferentiated memory concern.
setting_applicability: Direct for initial and specialist assessment, monitoring, care coordination, driving information, and carer support.
UAE_applicability: International NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review and local referral, driving, social-care, and support pathways.
recency_verification: The current official NG97 recommendations, reviewed 2025-10-24 with no update required, were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: NG97 remains the current official NICE dementia guideline.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped memory identity, patient and collateral history, progression, domains, daily impact, mood, medicine history, cognition, neurological and functional assessment, general blood tests, previous cognitive assessment, formal cognitive assessment, history, and referral fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports patient and collateral history, cognitive, behavioural and psychological symptoms, daily-life impact, physical and neurological examination, appropriate blood or urine tests, validated cognitive tools, functional informant tools, reversible causes, rapid progression, and specialist assessment.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped arranged follow-up, social support, and care-plan fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports flexible or prescheduled monitoring, review of emerging needs, a named coordinator, and documented care and support planning.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped driving and carer-support documentation fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented driving information and review of carer information, assessment, education, and support needs.

unresolved_source_gaps: Children and memory concerns outside suspected dementia are outside the exact source.

Head trauma, focal symptoms, falls, acute behaviour wording, blood pressure, ECG, specific individual laboratory tests, imaging, generic safety-netting, generic clinician plans, and fixed follow-up remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, cognitive interpretation, driving decisions, referral, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
